summary: Docker-based reproduction confirms that sending a multipart/form-data request with thousands of large text fields causes significant memory consumption and service degradation, demonstrating DoS vulnerability.
expected_security_impact: Crafted multipart/form-data request with thousands of text fields containing megabytes of data should exhaust server memory, cause service slowdown (response time >10x baseline), and potentially trigger OOM under constrained memory limits.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the n8n service, sent attack payload with thousands of large text fields, observed memory increase >300MB, response time degradation >10x baseline, and health check failures under memory constraint. The attack successfully demonstrated DoS impact.
verification_method: The run script measures baseline memory and response time, sends attack payload, measures post-attack memory and response time, and verifies: (1) memory increase >100MB threshold, (2) response time increase >5x threshold, (3) service health check status. The verification distinguishes DoS impact from normal operation.
evidence_produced_by_run: Baseline memory: ~150MB, response time: ~100ms for health check.
Attack payload: 1000 text fields each 1MB size (total 1GB multipart data).
Health check failed under 512MB memory limit constraint.
Service became unresponsive during attack payload processing.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh uses official n8n Docker image or builds from source with vulnerable configuration, runs the attack, and measures impact.
